stimulus.content: A primary source is created by someone who was there when an event happened. Examples include diaries, letters, and photographs from the time period. A secondary source is created later by someone who studied the event. Textbooks and encyclopedias are examples of secondary sources.

stem: Which item is an example of a primary source from the American Revolution?

choices.text: A textbook chapter about the war
A letter written by a soldier in 1778
A film made about George Washington

answer_explanation: A letter written by a soldier during the Revolution is a primary source because the writer was there at the time. Textbooks, websites, and films about the era are secondary sources created later.
